Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Mirror V2 — Streaming Speech, Product Visualization & Tests

## Context

The Mirror V2 page (`frontend/src/app/mirror-v2/page.tsx`) has the infrastructure for speech display and clothing visualization, but three gaps prevent it from working end-to-end:

1. **Speech text is invisible during streaming** — chunks accumulate in `responseAccumulatorRef` but `speechText` only updates at end-of-message (line 249). Users see nothing while Mira speaks.
2. **`present_items` results are silently dropped** — the `tool_result` handler (line 291) only checks for `type === "display_product"`, ignoring `type === "clothing_results"` from `present_items`.
3. **No fallback when flat lays or pose are missing** — `mapToClothingItems` filters out items without `cleaned_image_url`/`flat_image_url`, and `ClothingCanvas` requires `currentPose` (line 690). If Gemini fails or camera isn't available, nothing renders.

An existing `ProductCarousel` component (`frontend/src/components/mirror/ProductCarousel.tsx`) already handles card-based product display with gesture interactions — it just isn't wired into mirror-v2.

---

## Phase 0: Setup Git Worktree

- Create a new git worktree called `frontend-streaming` branching from current branch (`feature/full-merge`)
- All work happens in the worktree directory
- Command: `git worktree add ../mirrorless-frontend-streaming -b frontend-streaming`

---

## ~~Phase 1: Streaming Speech Display~~ (SKIPPED — will be addressed via prompt changes later)

---

## Phase 2: Product Visualization

**File: `frontend/src/app/mirror-v2/page.tsx`**

### 2a. Handle `clothing_results` type
- Import `ProductCarousel` and its `ProductCard` type
- Add `carouselItems` state (`ProductCard[]`)
- In `handleToolResult`: add handler for `type === "clothing_results"` — map items to `ProductCard[]` and set `carouselItems`
- Clear `carouselItems` on session start/end

### 2b. Fallback for `display_product` without flat lays
- In the existing `display_product` handler: when `mapToClothingItems` returns 0 items (flat lay generation failed), fall back to `ProductCarousel` by mapping raw items to `ProductCard[]`

### 2c. Add ProductCarousel to JSX
- Render `ProductCarousel` when `sessionActive && carouselItems.length > 0`
- Add `handleCarouselGesture` callback that emits `mirror_event` via Socket.io
- Hide `PriceStrip` when carousel is showing (avoids bottom-of-screen z-index conflict)

---

## Phase 3: Extract Testable Logic

To keep tests clean and fast (no component rendering), extract handler logic into pure functions:

**New file: `frontend/src/lib/process-tool-result.ts`**
- Extract `processToolResult(data)` — returns `{ canvasItems, carouselCards, priceInfo, outfitName }` or `null`
- Reuse `mapToClothingItems` internally
- Handles both `display_product` (with flat lay fallback) and `clothing_results`

Page.tsx calls this function in `handleToolResult` and uses the result to update state.

---

## Phase 4: Frontend Tests

All in `frontend/src/__tests__/`, following existing vitest + jsdom patterns.

### `mirror-v2-tool-result.test.ts`
- Tests the extracted `processToolResult` function
- Tests: `display_product` with flat lays → canvasItems, `display_product` without flat lays → carouselCards fallback, `clothing_results` → carouselCards, unknown type → null, empty items → null

### `speech-display.test.tsx`
- Component test with `@testing-library/react`
- Tests: renders text when visible, hides when not visible, shows streaming text, updates on rerender

---

## Phase 5: Backend Tests

All in `backend/tests/`, following existing pytest + asyncio patterns.

### `test_tool_execution.py` (single file for all three tools)
- **`_present_items`**: returns `clothing_results` payload, caps at 5 items, empty items returns error
- **`_display_product`**: returns `display_product` payload, Gemini import error is non-fatal (items proceed without flat lays), Gemini API failure is non-fatal, empty items returns error
- **`_send_voice_to_client`**: returns `voice_message` payload, defaults emotion to neutral, empty text returns error

Gemini failure tests mock `services.gemini_flatlay.generate_flat_lays_batch` with `AsyncMock(side_effect=Exception(...))`.

---

## Phase 6: Verification

1. `cd frontend && npm run build` — TypeScript compilation
2. `cd frontend && npx vitest run` — all frontend tests
3. `cd backend && pytest tests/test_tool_execution.py -v` — new backend tests
4. Manual: open `/mirror-v2` in browser, verify streaming text appears as Mira speaks

---

## Key Files

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `frontend/src/app/mirror-v2/page.tsx` | Modify — carousel state, ProductCarousel integration |
| `frontend/src/components/mirror/ProductCarousel.tsx` | Reuse as-is |
| `frontend/src/lib/process-tool-result.ts` | **New** — extracted handler logic |
| `frontend/src/lib/map-clothing-items.ts` | Read-only (reused by process-tool-result) |
| `frontend/src/__tests__/mirror-v2-tool-result.test.ts` | **New** |
| `backend/tests/test_tool_execution.py` | **New** |
| `backend/agent/tools.py` | Read-only (test target) |


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/johnathanmo/.REDACTED.jsonl

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
This plan tackles three common issues in real-time AI UIs: invisible streaming text, dropped tool results, and missing fallback rendering. The strategy of extracting logic into pure functions (`processToolResult`) before testing is a solid pattern — it avoids heavy component mounts in tests while keeping the page.tsx thin.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

   286→def _parse_price(price_str: str) -> float | None:
   287→    """Extract numeric price from string like '$595.00'."""
   288→    match = re.search(r"[\d,]+\.?\d*", price_str.replace(",", ""))
   289→    return float(match.group()) if match else None
   290→
   291→
   292→def _extract_brand(item: dict) -> str:
   293→    """Extract the actual clothing brand from item title or query_brand tag."""
   294→    if item.get("query_brand"):
   295→        return item["query_brand"]
   296→    title = item.get("title", "").lower()
   297→    for brand in POPULAR_BRANDS:
   298→        if brand.lower() in title:
   299→            return brand
   300→    return item.get("source", "Unknown")
   301→
   302→
   303→def _select_diverse_items(items: list, limit: int) -> list:
   304→    """Select items balanced across actual clothing brands using round-robin."""
   305→    by_brand = defaultdict(list)
   306→    for item in items:
   307→        by_brand[_extract_brand(item)].append(item)
   308→
   309→    selected = []
   310→    brand_lists = list(by_brand.values())
   311→    idx = 0
   312→    while len(selected) < limit and brand_lists:
   313→        brand_items = brand_lists[idx % len(brand_lists)]
   314→        if brand_items:
   315→            selected.append(brand_items.pop(0))
   316→        else:
   317→            brand_lists.pop(idx % len(brand_lists))
   318→            if not brand_lists:
   319→                break
   320→            continue
   321→        idx += 1
   322→
   323→    return selected
   324→
   325→
   326→# --- Event-driven mirror session tool execution ---
   327→
   328→
   329→async def execute_tool(tool_name: str, tool_input: dict, user_context: dict) -> dict:
   330→    """Execute a tool call and return the result.
   331→
   332→    Args:
   333→        tool_name: Name of the tool to execute.
   334→        tool_input: Input parameters from Claude.
   335→        user_context: Dict with user-specific data (oauth_token, user_id, etc.)
   336→
   337→    Returns:
   338→        Dict with tool results and optional frontend_payload for parallel broadcast.
   339→    """
   340→    if tool_name == "search_clothing":
   341→        return await _search_clothing(tool_input)
   342→    elif tool_name == "present_items":
   343→        return await _present_items(tool_input)
   344→    elif tool_name == "search_gmail":
   345→        return await _search_gmail(tool_input, user_context)
   346→    elif tool_name == "search_purchases":
   347→        return await _search_purchases(tool_input, user_context)
   348→    elif tool_name == "search_calendar":

   698→
   699→
   700→async def execute_give_recommendation(
   701→    tool_input: Dict, session_id: str
   702→) -> str:
   703→    """
   704→    Execute the give_recommendation tool.
   705→
   706→    Searches Serper for clothing items from the specified brands,
   707→    tags them as tops/bottoms, caches results, and returns formatted list.
   708→    """
   709→    brands = tool_input.get("brands", [])
   710→    gender = tool_input.get("gender", "mens")
   711→
   712→    # Filter to known clothing brands + fill with popular brands
   713→    valid_brands = [b for b in brands if is_clothing_brand(b)]
   714→    if not valid_brands:
   715→        valid_brands = brands[:3]  # Use whatever Claude provided
   716→
   717→    # Fill remaining slots with popular brands
   718→    seen = {b.lower() for b in valid_brands}
   719→    for b in POPULAR_BRANDS:
   720→        if len(valid_brands) >= 7:
   721→            break
   722→        if b.lower() not in seen:
   723→            valid_brands.append(b)
   724→            seen.add(b.lower())
   725→
   726→    # Check cache first
   727→    cached = serper_cache.get(session_id)
   728→    if cached:
   729→        return _format_clothing_for_claude(cached)
   730→
   731→    # Build and execute queries
   732→    brand_queries = build_brand_queries(valid_brands[:5], gender)
   733→
   734→    serper_api_key = os.getenv("SERPER_API_KEY")
   735→    if not serper_api_key:
   736→        return "Error: Serper API key not configured. Cannot search for clothing."
   737→
   738→    # Fetch tops and bottoms in parallel
   739→    tops_items, bottoms_items = await asyncio.gather(
   740→        fetch_clothing_batch(
   741→            brand_queries["tops"], serper_api_key, num_results_per_query=3
   742→        ),
   743→        fetch_clothing_batch(
   744→            brand_queries["bottoms"], serper_api_key, num_results_per_query=3
   745→        ),
   746→    )
   747→
   748→    # Tag items with category
   749→    for item in tops_items:
   750→        item["clothing_category"] = "top"
   751→    for item in bottoms_items:
   752→        item["clothing_category"] = "bottom"
   753→
   754→    all_items = tops_items + bottoms_items
   755→
   756→    # Cache results for session
   757→    serper_cache.set(session_id, all_items)
   758→
   759→    if not all_items:
   760→        return "No clothing items found. Try different brands or check back later."
   761→
   762→    # Select diverse subset
   763→    limited_tops = _select_diverse_items(
   764→        [i for i in all_items if i.get("clothing_category") == "top"], 25
   765→    )
   766→    limited_bottoms = _select_diverse_items(
   767→        [i for i in all_items if i.get("clothing_category") == "bottom"], 25
   768→    )
   769→
   770→    return _format_clothing_for_claude(limited_tops + limited_bottoms)
   771→
   772→
   773→def _format_clothing_for_claude(items: List[Dict]) -> str:
   774→    """Format clothing items into a readable string for Claude."""
   775→    tops = [i for i in items if i.get("clothing_category") == "top"]
   776→    bottoms = [i for i in items if i.get("clothing_category") == "bottom"]
   777→
   778→    def _format_section(section_items: List[Dict]) -> str:
   779→        text = ""
   780→        for item in section_items:
   781→            text += (
   782→                f"- **{item['title']}**\n"
   783→                f"  - Brand/Seller: {item['source']}\n"
   784→                f"  - Price: {item['price']}\n"
   785→                f"  - Rating: {item.get('rating', 'N/A')}\n"
   786→                f"  - Link: {item['link']}\n"
   787→                f"  - Image: {item['image_url']}\n"
   788→                f"  - Product ID: {item['product_id']}\n"
   789→            )
   790→        return text
   791→
   792→    result = f"Found {len(items)} clothing items:\n\n"
   793→    result += "IMPORTANT: Pick tops ONLY from the TOPS section and bottoms ONLY from the BOTTOMS section.\n\n"
   794→
   795→    if tops:
   796→        result += f"### TOPS ({len(tops)} items) — use these for the \"top\" slot:\n"
   797→        result += _format_section(tops)
   798→
   799→    if bottoms:
   800→        result += f"\n### BOTTOMS ({len(bottoms)} items) — use these for the \"bottom\" slot:\n"
   801→        result += _format_section(bottoms)
   802→
   803→    return result
   804→

"""Tests for queue: join, position calculation, and status transitions."""

import os
import uuid

import pytest
import pytest_asyncio
from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv(os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "..", ".env"))

from tests.test_database import NeonSQL, DATABASE_URL


@pytest_asyncio.fixture(scope="session")
async def db():
    return NeonSQL(DATABASE_URL)


@pytest_asyncio.fixture
async def queue_user(db):
    """Create a test user for queue tests, clean up after."""
    email = f"queue_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}@mirrorless.test"
    rows = await db.execute(
        "INSERT INTO users (name, email) VALUES ($1, $2) RETURNING id",
        ["Queue Tester", email],
    )
    user_id = rows[0]["id"]
    yield user_id
    await db.execute("DELETE FROM queue WHERE user_id = $1::uuid", [user_id])
    await db.execute("DELETE FROM users WHERE id = $1::uuid", [user_id])


async def test_join_queue_assigns_next_position(db, queue_user):
    """Joining the queue assigns the next available position."""
    max_pos = await db.fetchval("SELECT COALESCE(MAX(position), 0) FROM queue")
    expected_pos = int(max_pos) + 1

    rows = await db.execute(
        """
        INSERT INTO queue (user_id, position)
        VALUES ($1::uuid, $2)
        RETURNING id, position, status
        """,
        [queue_user, expected_pos],
    )
    assert rows[0]["position"] == expected_pos
    assert rows[0]["status"] == "waiting"


async def test_queue_total_ahead(db, queue_user):
    """Total ahead count is correct based on position."""
    # The user was already inserted in previous test; check count
    row = await db.fetchrow(
        "SELECT position FROM queue WHERE user_id = $1::uuid AND status = 'waiting'",
        [queue_user],
    )
    if not row:
        pytest.skip("No waiting queue entry for user")

    count = await db.fetchval(
        """
        SELECT COUNT(*) FROM queue
        WHERE status = 'waiting' AND position < $1
        """,
        [row["position"]],
    )
    assert int(count) >= 0


async def test_queue_status_transition(db, queue_user):
    """Queue entry can transition from waiting to active to completed."""
    row = await db.fetchrow(
        "SELECT id FROM queue WHERE user_id = $1::uuid AND status = 'waiting'",
        [queue_user],
    )
    if not row:
        pytest.skip("No waiting queue entry")

    queue_id = row["id"]

    # waiting -> active
    await db.execute(
        "UPDATE queue SET status = 'active' WHERE id = $1::uuid",
        [queue_id],
    )
    updated = await db.fetchrow("SELECT status FROM queue WHERE id = $1::uuid", [queue_id])
    assert updated["status"] == "active"

    # active -> completed
    await db.execute(
        "UPDATE queue SET status = 'completed' WHERE id = $1::uuid",
        [queue_id],
    )
    updated = await db.fetchrow("SELECT status FROM queue WHERE id = $1::uuid", [queue_id])
    assert updated["status"] == "completed"


async def test_duplicate_join_returns_existing(db):
    """If a user already has a waiting entry, no duplicate is created."""
    email = f"dup_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}@mirrorless.test"
    rows = await db.execute(
        "INSERT INTO users (name, email) VALUES ($1, $2) RETURNING id",
        ["Dup Tester", email],
    )
    user_id = rows[0]["id"]

    max_pos = await db.fetchval("SELECT COALESCE(MAX(position), 0) FROM queue")
    pos = int(max_pos) + 1

    # First join
    await db.execute(
        "INSERT INTO queue (user_id, position) VALUES ($1::uuid, $2)",
        [user_id, pos],
    )

    # Check existing before inserting again
    existing = await db.execute(
        """
        SELECT id, position, status FROM queue
        WHERE user_id = $1::uuid AND status IN ('waiting', 'active')
        """,
        [user_id],
    )
    assert len(existing) == 1
    assert existing[0]["position"] == pos

    # Clean up
    await db.execute("DELETE FROM queue WHERE user_id = $1::uuid", [user_id])
    await db.execute("DELETE FROM users WHERE id = $1::uuid", [user_id])

"""Tests for agent tool execution functions: _present_items and _display_product."""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, patch


@pytest.fixture
def _mock_neon_db():
    """Prevent any actual database connections during tool tests."""
    with patch("agent.tools.NeonHTTPClient"):
        yield


# ── present_items ──


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_present_items_returns_clothing_results_payload():
    from agent.tools import _present_items

    items = [
        {"title": "Blue Shirt", "price": "$49", "image_url": "https://img/1.jpg", "link": "https://a.com", "source": "Nike"},
        {"title": "Black Pants", "price": "$79", "image_url": "https://img/2.jpg", "link": "https://b.com", "source": "Zara"},
    ]
    result = await _present_items({"items": items})

    assert result["presented"] == 2
    assert result["frontend_payload"]["type"] == "clothing_results"
    assert len(result["frontend_payload"]["items"]) == 2


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_present_items_caps_at_five():
    from agent.tools import _present_items

    items = [{"title": f"Item {i}", "price": "$10", "image_url": f"https://img/{i}.jpg", "link": "#", "source": "X"} for i in range(10)]
    result = await _present_items({"items": items})

    assert result["presented"] == 5
    assert len(result["frontend_payload"]["items"]) == 5

    from agent.tools import _present_items

    result = await _present_items({"items": []})

    assert "error" in result
    assert result["presented"] == 0

    from agent.tools import _present_items

    from agent.tools import _display_product

    items = [
        {"title": "T-Shirt", "image_url": "https://img/t.jpg", "product_id": "p1"},
        {"title": "Jeans", "image_url": "https://img/j.jpg", "product_id": "p2"},

    assert result["displayed"] == 2
    assert result["frontend_payload"]["type"] == "display_product"
    assert result["frontend_payload"]["outfit_name"] == "Casual"
    # p1 got a flat lay, p2 did not
    assert result["items"][0].get("cleaned_image_url") == "data:image/png;base64,flat1"
